page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6.2xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz. The instance is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio. It does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage options. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The single-core performance of the Intel Xeon processor is categorized as weak, as demonstrated by stress-ng single-core and gzip compression tests. However, multi-core performance scales to average levels, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 6986.33 and a PassMark CPU score of 8224.85. Memory bandwidth remains consistently average across both cached small-block reads at 390.01 GB/sec and uncached large-block writes at 36.78 GB/sec. Database operations, such as Redis SET operations, also perform at an average tier, reaching 6.6 million operations per second.

The primary tradeoff of the ecs.c6.2xlarge is its weak single-threaded execution speed and lack of bundled local storage, which limits its utility for single-core intensive tasks or high-speed local caching. However, the dedicated CPU allocation ensures predictable performance for multi-threaded workloads. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the instance offers a balanced compute-to-memory ratio for workloads that require dedicated physical cores without the premium of high-capacity RAM. It is appropriate for multi-threaded applications, medium-scale database operations, and general compute tasks that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz, paired with 16.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark data shows weak single-core performance, but average multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities, including a PassMark CPU score of 8224.85 and 6.6 million Redis SET operations per second. The instance offers a qualitative balance of dedicated compute resources without the overhead of excess memory. It is suitable for multi-threaded workloads, medium-scale database operations, and distributed computing tasks that rely on network-attached storage.
